evals.prompt: I want to move my Xamarin.Android barcode scanning app off Xamarin. Here is my project file (LegacyAndroid.csproj), its packages.config, and MainActivity.cs. Migrate it to .NET for Android.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects a legacy Xamarin.Android project, converts the .csproj to SDK-style with TargetFramework net8.0-android and SupportedOSPlatformVersion 24, swaps Scandit.DataCapture.*.Xamarin packages for the suffix-less .NET packages pinned to a version fetched from nuget.org, adds an [Application] MainApplication subclass calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() (required by SDK 8), and hands off to barcode-capture-net-android for the call sites. It does NOT rename BarcodeCapture.Create or the IBarcodeCaptureListener callbacks.

assertions.text: The project is converted to SDK-style with <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
<TargetFramework>net8.0-android</TargetFramework> (or the confirmed net* version) is set
SupportedOSPlatformVersion is set to at least 24
Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Xamarin is replaced by Scandit.DataCapture.Core (the .Xamarin suffix is dropped) in code blocks
Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Xamarin is replaced by Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode in code blocks
The answer instructs fetching the latest stable version from nuget.org rather than hardcoding a guessed version
ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() is added (SDK 8 explicit initialization)
The skill hands off to barcode-capture-net-android for the Scandit call sites
BarcodeCapture.Create( is preserved (not renamed) in code blocks

evals.prompt: Migrate my Xamarin.iOS app to .NET for iOS. Attached: AppDelegate.ios.cs and Info.plist.
evals.expected_output: The skill converts the project to SDK-style with TargetFramework net8.0-ios and SupportedOSPlatformVersion 15.0, keeps NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, swaps the Scandit Xamarin packages for the suffix-less .NET packages, adds ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching before the root view controller is created, and hands off to barcode-capture-net-ios.

assertions.text: <TargetFramework>net8.0-ios</TargetFramework> (or the confirmed net* version) is set
SupportedOSPlatformVersion is set to at least 15.0
NSCameraUsageDescription is preserved in Info.plist
ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() is added in FinishedLaunching before the root view controller is created
The Scandit packages are swapped to the suffix-less .NET IDs (Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode) in code blocks
The skill hands off to barcode-capture-net-ios for the Scandit call sites

evals.prompt: Convert my Xamarin.Forms app to .NET MAUI. Here are FormsApp.csproj, App.xaml.cs, and BadgeViewRenderer.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a single multi-target MAUI SDK-style project (net8.0-android;net8.0-ios, UseMaui true), moves startup into MauiProgram.CreateMauiApp with the .UseScanditCore(...).UseScanditBarcode() builder chain, updates Xamarin.Forms namespaces to Microsoft.Maui, adds BOTH the plain and *.Maui Scandit packages, flags the BadgeView custom renderer as a manual migration to a MAUI handler, and flags the DependencyService IAudioService usage as a manual migration to DI. It does NOT hand-call ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() in MAUI.

assertions.text: A multi-target MAUI project is produced with <UseMaui>true</UseMaui> and TargetFrameworks including net8.0-android and net8.0-ios
Startup is moved into MauiProgram.CreateMauiApp with a .UseScanditCore( and .UseScanditBarcode( builder chain
Xamarin.Forms namespaces/usings are updated to Microsoft.Maui in code blocks
Both Scandit.DataCapture.Core and Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui packages are added
Both Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui packages are added
The BadgeView custom renderer is flagged as a manual migration to a MAUI handler
The DependencyService usage is flagged as a manual migration to dependency injection
The skill hands off to barcode-capture-maui for the Scandit call sites

evals.prompt: My old Xamarin app uses this: LegacyPicker.packages.config. Can you migrate the Scandit part to .NET?
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes Scandit.BarcodePicker.Xamarin as the legacy v5 Barcode Picker (not the Data Capture SDK), explains there is no direct package swap, and flags it as a manual reintegration onto Barcode Capture or SparkScan, handing off to barcode-capture-net-* / barcode-capture-maui or sparkscan-* rather than pretending a drop-in replacement exists.

assertions.text: Scandit.BarcodePicker.Xamarin is identified as the legacy v5 Barcode Picker, not the Data Capture SDK
The answer states there is no direct package/API equivalent and that a reintegration is required
The item is flagged as manual-only rather than presented as an automatic package swap
The skill hands off to a Barcode Capture or SparkScan implementation skill for the reintegration

evals.prompt: I already converted my Xamarin.Android csproj to net8.0-android and swapped the Scandit packages last week, but I never finished. Continue the migration without redoing what's done.
evals.expected_output: The skill re-runs detection first, recognizes the SDK-style net8.0-android project and the already-swapped .NET Scandit packages, skips those steps, and continues with the remaining work (e.g. SDK 8 initialization and call-site verification), reflecting the resumable/idempotent behavior.

assertions.text: The skill re-runs detection before making changes
The skill recognizes the already-completed steps (SDK-style csproj, swapped packages) and does not redo them
The skill continues with the remaining steps rather than restarting the whole migration
